page_title: Super visa for parents and grandparents: After you apply - Canada.ca

h3: While you’re in Canada and when re-entering

sections.heading: On this page
items: Find out what happens after you apply for a super visa.
bullets: Processing your super visa application
If your application is approved
When you arrive in Canada
When you enter Canada
Living in Canada
If you change your name

sections.heading: Processing your super visa application
items: We’ll check your application to make sure you have all the documents you need and that you paid the fees.
If your application is incomplete, it may be refused.
We may also ask you to
We’ll tell you what to do if you need to do any of these.
Processing times depend on the visa office and on whether you need to take any extra steps (listed above).
bullets: go to an interview with one of our officials
send more information
get a medical exam valid medical exams are required for all applicants
valid medical exams are required for all applicants
get a police certificate

sections.heading: Application status
items: Use the application status tracker to get updates about your application. To create an account, you’ll need your
Check your application status
bullets: unique client identifier (UCI)
application number

sections.heading: If your application is approved
items: You’ll receive a letter with instructions to submit your passport to a visa application centre (VAC) to receive a visa. You cannot have your visa printed in Canada. If you’re from a visa-exempt country , we’ll issue you a letter to give to a border services officer when you arrive in Canada.
If your application is refused, we’ll send you an explanation.

sections.heading: Identity check
items: When you arrive, we’ll check your identity to make sure that you’re the same person who was approved to travel to Canada.
bullets: If you enter Canada at 1 of 10 major Canadian airports Your fingerprints will be checked automatically at a primary inspection kiosk . The system will check your identity against the information collected when your application was submitted.
Your fingerprints will be checked automatically at a primary inspection kiosk .
The system will check your identity against the information collected when your application was submitted.

If you enter Canada at smaller airports and all land ports of entry Your fingerprints may be checked if we refer you to a secondary inspection , where a border services officer will use a fingerprint verification device to check your fingerprints.
Your fingerprints may be checked if we refer you to a secondary inspection , where a border services officer will use a fingerprint verification device to check your fingerprints.

sections.heading: Have your documents ready
items: When you arrive, you’ll be greeted by an officer from the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA).

The officer will ask to see your passport or travel documents. You should also bring the documents you used for your super visa application (opens in a new tab) . Make sure these documents aren’t packed in your luggage.
A valid super visa and travel document (passport, titre de voyage or Certificate of Identity) does not guarantee that you can enter Canada.
Be prepared to show your proof of paid health insurance to a border services officer when you enter Canada.

sections.heading: When you enter Canada
items: If you pass the identity check and meet the entry requirements , the border services officer will stamp your passport, allowing you to stay in Canada for 5 years.
You won’t be allowed into Canada if you give false or incomplete information. You must convince the officer that
Make sure that your passport and health insurance are valid during your stay in Canada.
bullets: you’re eligible for entry into Canada
you’ll leave Canada at the end of your approved stay

sections.heading: Entry requirements
items: To visit Canada, you’ll need to meet the following basic requirements:
Some people are inadmissible. This means they’re not allowed to enter Canada. Some reasons you may be inadmissible to Canada include involvement in:
You can also be inadmissible for security, health or financial reasons.
Find out more about inadmissibility .
bullets: have a valid travel document, such as a passport
be in good health
provide proof of adequate health insurance for a minimum of one year from entry to Canada
continue to meet the requirements of the super visa
continue to meet the admissibility requirements
have no criminal or immigration-related convictions
convince an immigration officer that you have ties to your home country that will take you back examples include: a job, home, financial assets or family
examples include: a job, home, financial assets or family
convince an immigration officer that you’ll leave Canada at the end of your visit
criminal activity
human rights violations
organized crime

sections.heading: Living in Canada
items: Canada may be very different from your home country, which means there’s a lot to learn about it before you arrive.
Get to know Canada , including its climate, people, government and way of life.

sections.heading: Work or study in Canada
items: A super visa doesn’t authorize you to work or study in Canada. Most visitors to Canada need a permit to work or study in Canada.

sections.heading: While you’re in Canada and when re-entering
items: As a super visa holder, you need to
bullets: maintain your health insurance while in Canada proof of valid health insurance will be required on re-entry
proof of valid health insurance will be required on re-entry
make sure your passport is valid while you’re in Canada

sections.heading: If you change your name
items: If you legally changed your name after you applied for a super visa, you must submit a request to change your name. What you need depends on whether you’re applying to change your name from inside or outside Canada.
Get the right documents to change your name

eligibility_signals: proof of ties to home country
